questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Having a feeling of guilt
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The option that can be used as a one-word substitute for "Having a feeling of guilt" is: Remorse Remorse: A deep feeling of regret, guilt, or sorrow for one's actions or wrongdoing. Restitution: The act of restoring or compensating for something that has been lost, damaged, or taken away. Repugnance: A strong feeling of disgust, aversion, or opposition towards something. Refulgence: The state or quality of being brightly shining or radiant.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom that can be substituted in the following sentence. Let us know what you think i̲n̲ ̲b̲l̲a̲c̲k̲ ̲a̲n̲d̲ ̲w̲h̲i̲t̲e̲.
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} in black and white paint
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom "in black and white" in the sentence "Let us know what you think in black and white" is: In writing The idiom "in black and white" means something that is clearly written or documented, leaving no room for doubt or misunderstanding.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the correct passive form of the given sentence. He drank a lot of coffee before the marathon.
options: A lot of coffee was drunk by him before the marathon.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} A lot of coffee had been drunk by him before the marathon.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} A lot of coffee was drunken by him before the marathon.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} A lot of coffee is drunk by him before the marathon.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct passive form of the given sentence "He drank a lot of coffee before the marathon" is: A lot of coffee was drunk by him before the marathon. Active voice ---- Passive voice Subject + V2 + Object ---- Object + was/were + V3 + by + subject Therefore, the corrected answer would be: “A lot of coffee was drunk by him before the marathon.”

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. Social economics is a meta-discipline in which economics is e̲n̲t̲r̲e̲n̲c̲h̲e̲d̲ in social, political and cultural behaviours.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The most appropriate antonym of the underlined word "entrenched" in the given sentence is: Transient Entrenched: Firmly established and difficult to change; deeply ingrained. Transient: Lasting only for a short time; temporary. Averted: Prevented or turned away; stopped from happening. Startling: Very surprising, astonishing, or remarkable. Entrusted: Given the responsibility of doing something or caring for someone.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words in the following sentence. Sita seems n̲o̲t̲ ̲t̲o̲ ̲b̲e̲ ̲a̲f̲f̲e̲c̲t̲e̲d̲ ̲b̲y̲ ̲t̲h̲e̲ ̲p̲l̲e̲a̲s̲u̲r̲e̲s̲ ̲a̲n̲d̲ ̲p̲a̲i̲n̲s̲ ̲o̲f̲ ̲l̲i̲f̲e̲.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The one-word substitute for the underlined group of words "not to be affected by the pleasures and pains of life" is: Stoic Stoic: Enduring pain and hardship without showing feelings or complaining; unaffected by pleasure or pain. Active: Engaging or ready to engage in physically energetic pursuits; taking action or doing something. Strict: Demanding that rules concerning behavior are obeyed and observed; rigid in enforcing rules. Passive: Accepting or allowing what happens or what others do, without active response or resistance.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate synonym of the bracketed word to fill in the blank. Your words seemed more like a demand rather than a _________ (request).
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The most appropriate synonym of the bracketed word "request" to fill in the blank is: Plea Plea: An earnest request or appeal. Order: A command or instruction given by someone in authority. Compromise: An agreement or settlement of a dispute where each side makes concessions. Contract: A written or spoken agreement that is enforceable by law.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. The students usually asks / for a bonus time of / thirty minutes for / completing their projects.
options: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} for a bonus time of
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The segment that contains a grammatical error is: The students usually asks The correct form should be "The students usually ask" because "students" is plural, and the verb should agree with the plural subject. Thus, the corrected sentence should be: "The students usually ask for a bonus time of thirty minutes for completing their projects."

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Choose the incorrectly spelt word.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The incorrectly spelt word is: Subsistance The correct spelling is "subsistence." Benchmark: A standard or point of reference against which things may be compared or assessed. Subsistence: The action or fact of maintaining or supporting oneself, especially at a minimal level. Knight: A man who served his sovereign or lord as a mounted soldier in armor. Aggravate: To make (a problem, injury, or offense) worse or more serious.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The incorrectly spelt word is: Extarnal The correct spelling is "external." Angel: A spiritual being believed to act as a messenger of God, typically depicted as a winged figure. Decide: To make a choice or come to a conclusion about something. Musk: A strong-smelling substance secreted by certain animals, traditionally used in perfumery. External: Relating to or situated on the outside; coming from outside.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. The students were w̲a̲r̲n̲e̲d̲ ̲o̲n̲ coming late to school.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence is: warned against The phrase "warned against" is used to indicate caution or advice given to prevent someone from doing something. It implies that the students were advised not to engage in the specific behavior of coming late to school.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. One of the major challenges / he faced during his / undergraduate days were / the shortage of money.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The segment that contains a grammatical error is: undergraduate days were The correct form should be "undergraduate days was" because the subject of the clause "One of the major challenges" is singular, and therefore the verb should also be singular. Therefore, the corrected answer would be: “One of the major challenges he faced during his undergraduate days was the shortage of money.”

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. The singer complained to the police that she was p̲u̲r̲p̲o̲r̲t̲e̲d̲l̲y̲ attacked back stage immediately after the concert.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is a: Impossibly Purportedly: Allegedly or supposedly; according to what is claimed or said by some people, but not necessarily proven. Impossibly: In a way that is not possible; beyond the bounds of possibility. Severely: In a manner that is very harsh or intense; to a great degree. Dramatically: In a striking or impressive manner; with noticeable or significant change. Maliciously: In a manner showing a desire to harm others or to see others suffer; with ill will.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. I felt ________ elated at the news.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The most appropriate option to fill in the blank is: strangely Typically: In a way that is characteristic of a particular person or thing; usually. Strangely: In a way that is unusual or surprising; oddly. Commonly: Generally or ordinarily; in most cases. Customarily: According to the customs or usual practices associated with a particular society, place, or set of circumstances.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the correct direct form of the given sentence. Meena said that her parents were getting divorced.
options: Meena said, "My parents will be getting divorced."
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Meena said, "My parents were getting divorced."
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Meena said, "My parents get divorced."
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Meena said, "My parents are getting divorced."
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is d: Meena said, "My parents are getting divorced." Direct speech ---- Indirect speech Present continuous ---- Past continuous Therefore, the correct direct form is: Meena said, "My parents are getting divorced."

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Either he is mad, or he feigns ________.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The most appropriate option to fill in the blank is: madness Madness: The state of being mentally ill, especially severely; extremely foolish behavior. Sadness: The condition or quality of being sad; feeling sorrow or unhappiness. Calmness: The state or quality of being free from agitation or excitement; tranquility. Happiness: The state of being happy; feeling or showing pleasure or contentment.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A person who criticises traditional beliefs and customs
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The one-word substitute for "a person who criticizes traditional beliefs and customs" is: Iconoclast Iconoclast: A person who attacks or criticizes cherished beliefs or institutions. Absconder: A person who leaves secretly and hides, often to avoid arrest or prosecution. Heretic: A person holding an opinion at odds with what is generally accepted, especially in religion. Apostle: A vigorous and pioneering advocate or supporter of a particular cause.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Rearrange the parts of the sentence in correct order. The absence of P. data on the migrant workers was Q. formulating effective policy decisions and R. preventing the governments from S. strategies for the welfare of migrant workers
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct order of the parts to form a coherent sentence is: PRQS The sentence should logically flow to describe what the absence of data on migrant workers was causing. Here's how PRQS forms a coherent sentence: P: "data on the migrant workers was" R: "preventing the governments from" Q: "formulating effective policy decisions and" S: "strategies for the welfare of migrant workers"

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Red tape
options: Official rules and bureaucracy that make it difficult to do something
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Have a negative amount in your bank balance
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Catch someone at the moment they are doing something wrong
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Something unimportant that takes attention away from the main subject
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The most appropriate meaning of the idiom "red tape" is: Official rules and bureaucracy that make it difficult to do something Red tape: Excessive bureaucracy or adherence to official rules and formalities, especially in public administration, that hinders or complicates the efficient operation of procedures or tasks.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the given sentence. The young fellow was innocent and without g̲u̲i̲l̲e̲.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The correct answer is a: Deceit Guile: the ability to be clever but by using dishonest means. Deceit: The act of deceiving someone by concealing or misrepresenting the truth. Morality: Principles concerning the distinction between right and wrong or good and bad behavior. Greed: Intense and selfish desire for something, especially wealth, power, or food. Pride: A feeling of deep pleasure or satisfaction derived from one's own achievements.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. They flew a kite in the evening.
options: In the evening, a kite have been flown by them.
*{overflow-wrap: break-word;} A kite was flown by them in the evening.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Being constant (1) _________ repeating a task or action, or performing an activity without a stop or interval. Consistency can never be attained in a sporadic or (2) _______ manner. When you perform anything regularly or constantly, you will not only build expertise in that area, but you will also gain a better (3) ________ of it. Because of the greater amount of effort put in over a longer period of time, this enhanced comprehension is (4) ________. As a result, consistency is the (5) _________ that allows anyone to consistently put in the effort. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1: Entails Envelops: Surrounds completely; covers or encloses. Initiates: Begins or starts something. Entails: Involves or necessitates as a necessary consequence. Figure: A numerical symbol, especially one of Arabic numerals.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. When you perform anything regularly or constantly, you will not only build expertise in that area, but you will also gain a better (3) ________ of it. Because of the greater amount of effort put in over a longer period of time, this enhanced comprehension is (4) ________. As a result, consistency is the (5) _________ that allows anyone to consistently put in the effort. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2: Intermittent Intermittent: Occurring at irregular intervals; not continuous or steady. Casual: Relaxed and unconcerned; not formal or serious. Focus: Concentration of attention or energy on something. Discipline: The practice of training people to obey rules or a code of behavior.

questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3: Comprehension Ignorance: Lack of knowledge or information about something. questions.question: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Being constant (1) _________ repeating a task or action, or performing an activity without a stop or interval. Consistency can never be attained in a sporadic or (2) _______ manner. When you perform anything regularly or constantly, you will not only build expertise in that area, but you will also gain a better (3) ________ of it. Because of the greater amount of effort put in over a longer period of time, this enhanced comprehension is (4) ________. As a result, consistency is the (5) _________ that allows anyone to consistently put in the effort. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5: resistance Utensil: A tool, container, or other article, especially for household use; typically used in cooking or serving food.
